title: [LLVM][InstCombine][AArch64] sve.insr(splat(x), x) ==> splat(x)
summary:
The developers generalized a single-case optimization into a reusable pattern by identifying the core condition: an `insr` intrinsic inserting a value into a splat vector where the inserted value matches the splat constant. The original test only handled a constant `5` inserted into a splat of `5`. The generalization extracts the splat value via `getSplatValue(Vec)` and compares it to the inserted operand (`II.getOperand(1)`). If equal, the `insr` is redundant and the vector can be returned directly.

This transforms a hardcoded constant match into a generic value equivalence check, enabling the optimization to handle:
- Different constants (e.g., `3` into splat of `5`)
- Variable values (same or different)
- Floating-point types
- The `zeroinitializer` splat case

The approach demonstrates converting a specific instance into a general rule by isolating the invariant condition (value equality) and using existing IR analysis utilities (`getSplatValue`). This pattern is applicable to other intrinsics where redundant operations can be eliminated based on operand relationships.
